DECLARATION OF MARY BETH MCCARTHY IN SUPP OF OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S ANTI SLAPP MTN TO STRIKE FILED BY PLAINTIFF HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. F/K/A KAISER CEMENT , AND KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT JOHN WALLACE'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. ALL OF THE CLAIMS ALLEGED BY THE PLAINTIFF ARISE FROM DEFENDANT'S ACCEPTANCE OF EMPLOYMENT WITH BRAYTON PURCELL ADVERSE TO PLAINTIFFS MISREPRESENTING ASBESTOS BODILY INJURY CLAIMANTS AND NOT FROM ANY PROTECTED ACTIVITY. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED BECAUSE THIS CASE IS AT LEAST ARGUABLY DISTINGUISHABLE FROM UNITED STATES FIRE INSURANCE, FREEMAN AND BENASRA BECAUSE OF THE BREADTH OF THE RELIEF SOUGHT BY PLAINTIFFS. HOWEVER, THE FOREGOING LINE OF CASES SHOULD HAVE, AT THE VERY LEAST, BEEN DISCUSSED IN DEFENDANT'S INITIAL MEMORANDUM. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-23-2012 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-26-2012 Continued To Nov-07-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-30-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PHILIP E. COOK & ATTY. JASON CORBITT WRIGHT OF JONES DAY, TEL. NO. (213) 489-3939 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL P. BRADLEY & ATTY. TANIS J. LEUTHOLD OF MURPHY PEARSON & FEENEY,
TEL. NO. 788-1900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JOHN WALLACE'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS Denied. All of the claims alleged by the plaintiff arise from Defendant's acceptance of employment with Brayton Purcell adverse to plaintiffs misrepresenting asbestos bodily injury claimants and not from any protected activity. Plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions is denied because this case is at least arguably distinguishable from United States Fire Insurance, Freeman and Benasra because of the breadth of the relief sought by plaintiffs. However, the foregoing line of cases should have, at the very least, been discussed in defendant's initial memorandum. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 = (302/HEK)
